judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all four questions with matching command-level rigor; they are comparable, with Report 2 adding marginal extra rigor via patch-id verification.

report_1.evidence: Base branch: `main`. ... targeting the stale `feat/weighted-scorer` branch would base the PR on a branch whose content main already contains and which will presumably be deleted.

report_2.evidence: Base branch: `main`. ... the branch this work was actually built on, `feat/weighted-scorer`, is effectively dead as a base: its content already landed on `main` via the squash commit `1971f08` "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)". Targeting `feat/weighted-scorer` would stack a PR on a branch whose merge already happened

report_1.evidence: feat/pairwise-metric was created from the tip of feat/weighted-scorer at ac9d505 ... Main gained a single commit `1971f08` "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" ... Because the merge was a squash, git cannot recognize W1–W3 as already-merged

report_2.evidence: feat/pairwise-metric` was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505` ... `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` says no — `ac9d505` is not in `main`'s history — yet `git diff main feat/weighted-scorer` is empty and the patch-ids match, because PR #42 squash-merged the identical content as the single commit `1971f08`

report_1.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py` — the entire rewrite to weighted scoring ... So **no, not all of it is pairwise-metric work.** ... They appear because the diff is computed from the merge base `65204ad`, which predates the squash merge

report_2.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py` +13/−3 (the full weighted-metrics rewrite ... The entire `scoring/scorer.py` change and the `overlap()` function are `feat/weighted-scorer`'s W1–W3 work. It appears in the diff because the merge base with `main` is the old fork point `65204ad`

report_1.evidence: `git fetch origin` and confirm `origin/main` is still `1971f08` ... Rebase only the branch's own commits onto main: `git rebase --onto main ac9d505 feat/pairwise-metric`. ... `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` now equals the 5-line two-dot diff

report_2.evidence: Rebase only the two pairwise commits onto the current trunk tip: `git rebase --onto main ac9d505 feat/pairwise-metric`. ... `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` now equals the 5-insertion two-dot diff from question 3 — the diff is scoped to only the files the pairwise work meant to touch
